text:
REGIME — Broad risk-on grind, defensives led, metals lagged, vol collapsed.

INDICES — VIX 24.2, falling. QQQ +0.60%, IWM +0.43%, SPY +0.47%, DIA +0.37%. Small caps (IJR +0.54%) edged large caps (SPY +0.47%). VIXY dropped -2.45%, the sharpest cross-asset move, confirming fear unwinding.

SECTORS — XLP +0.94%, XLY +0.82%, XLE +0.73%, XLF +0.71%, XLK +0.58%, XLI +0.51%, XLRE +0.36%, XLC +0.05%, XLV -0.36%, XLU -0.37%, XLB -0.38%. Top industries: MSOS +6.23%, IBIT +4.08%. Bottom: TAN -1.26%, LIT -1.42%.

THE 7s — Defense +1.25%, Fab +1.22%, Housing +1.08%, Toll Road +1.00%, Semi +0.84%, Bank +0.80%, Industrial +0.66%, Energy +0.57%, New Staples +0.51%, Old Staples +0.45%, Insurance +0.44%, Freight +0.35%, Cloud +0.19%, Old Metals +0.05%, Medtech -0.16%, Power -0.27%, Health -0.39%, Buildout -0.40%, Software -0.63%, New Metals -0.85%. Strongest ticker in Defense 7 not specified in data; weakest in New Metals 7 not individually named.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed 54.4, Neutral. Top movers: MSOS +6.23% (Industries), IBIT +4.08% (Industries), WGMI +2.63% (Industries). 20d momentum best sector: XLE +6.19%; worst: XLY -4.53%. Best basket 20d: Energy +8.57%; worst: Software -10.72%. Powell's comments easing tightening fears supported QQQ and XLK as tech outperformed bonds on the session.

WATCH — EATZ: +2.02%, volume 10.5x average, extreme vol spike warrants momentum follow-through check. IBIT: +4.08%, crypto leading on risk-on day with VIX falling, watch for continuation. IJK/IJS: 8x and 6.2x vol spikes on small/mid value ETFs with modest price gains — divergence between volume surge and muted return flags potential rotation setup.
